Spanish Version(Español)

Environmental Justice

Purpose

The Environmental Justice Program within the Secretariat of Justice and Peace educates and calls Catholics to a deeper reverence and respect for God's creation and encourages Catholics to respond to environmental challenges, especially as they disproportionately affect our poor and vulnerable neighbors across the United States and the world.

Laudato Si' Week 2026

Laudato Si’ Week (May 17-24) is an eight-day campaign held annually in May to mark the anniversary of Pope Francis’ landmark encyclical on creation care. Sign up to receive daily emails throughout Laudato Si' Week below.

Sign up for daily emails here

Resources on Laudato Si'

Celebrating 10 Years of Laudato Si'

Laudato Si' is the appeal from Pope Francis addressed to "every person living on this planet" for an inclusive dialogue about how we are shaping the future of our planet. Celebrate the 10th anniversary of Laudato Si' during Laudato Si' Week (May 24-31) and throughout the year. 

USCCB Statement on 10th Anniversary of Laudato Si'

Archbishop Timothy P. Broglio, president of the USCCB, reflects on ten years of Laudato Si'.

Bishops' Letter to Young People

Archbishop Borys Gudziak and Bishop A. Elias Zaidan write a special letter to young people for the 10th anniversary of Laudato Si'.

Summary of Activities of the U.S. Church in Response to Laudato Si’

See examples of activities of the U.S. Church in Response to Laudato Si'.

Examples of Laudato Si' Activities in the Dioceses

See examples of activities to implement Laudato Si’ happening in dioceses around the country.

Ten Years of U.S. Catholic Engagement on Laudato Si'

The following is a snapshot of how the Church in the United States has taken action over the decade to protect our common home through education, conversion, advocacy and more.

Explore Laudato Si' 

Find resources including a discussion guide, bulletin inserts, videos, and statements on the Laudato Si’ encyclical.

An Invitation to Ecological Conversion for U.S. Catholics

The Committees on Domestic Justice and Human Development and International Justice and Peace issued a joint statement for Lent on ecological conversion.  

Read the statement here

New Resource from the Holy See on Integral Ecology in the Life of the Family

Integral ecology in the life of the family is a new document to educate about the care of Creation and human life from the Dicastery for Promoting Integral Human Development and the Dicastery for Laity, Family and Life.

Learn More Here

"We are faced not with two separate crises, one environmental and the other social, but rather with one complex crisis which is both social and environmental. Strategies for a solution demand an integrated approach to combating poverty, restoring dignity to the excluded, and at the same time protecting nature." 

Pope Francis
Laudato Si' no. 139

About USCCB

The United States Conference of Catholic Bishops’ (USCCB’s) mission is to encounter the mercy of Christ and to accompany His people with joy.
